content_preview.title: Rice and Weizmann Physicists Identify the Electronic Agents Behind Strange Metallicity in a Flat-Band Kagome Metal
content_preview.summary: Using atomic-resolution spectroscopy on Ni3In, researchers from Rice and Weizmann experimentally confirmed compact molecular orbitals as the drivers of flat-band quantum criticality.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Physicists at Rice University and the Weizmann Institute of Science have identified the fundamental electronic structures responsible for exotic quantum behavior in a class of materials that has long puzzled condensed matter physicists. The team used atomic-resolution spectroscopy to directly visualize what they describe as the governing "agents" of flat-band quantum materials, providing an experimental foundation for theories linking topology, electron correlation, and high-tempera...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-written Briefing with strong technical depth appropriate for a condensed matter physics story. Structure is clear, terminology accurate, and the significance section correctly situates the finding within the broader strange metallicity problem.
editor_notes.source_verification: source-0 (Rice News) and source-3 (Phys.org via Archive.org) confirmed all major quotes and facts. source-2 (bioengineer.org) confirmed the 'spatial profiles precisely matching' quote. source-4 (Nature Physics) confirmed paper title, abstract, author list, and publication metadata. source-1 (EurekAlert) returned a 202 JavaScript-disabled page — content unverifiable from snapshot; EurekAlert-attributed claims are supported by other sources.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: One date error found: article states the paper was 'published in Nature Physics on March 20, 2026' but the Nature paper metadata shows datePublished as 2026-03-17 (March 17). The March 20 date is the Rice News press release date, not the paper publication date. All other specifics verified: Ni3In material, Qimiao Si's title and affiliation, Haim Beidenkopf as corresponding author, Mounica Mahankali as co-first author, Jean C. Souza as first author, paper title, and all quotes.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. No sensationalism or editorializing.
editor_notes.originality: Unique story not covered in recent archive. Physics paper from March 2026 not previously published on The Machine Herald.
concerns: Article states paper published 'March 20, 2026' — Nature metadata shows March 17, 2026. Rice News press release (March 20) was incorrectly used as the paper publication date.
recommendations: Add bioengineer.org to source allowlist — legitimate science news aggregator with accurate content verified against primary sources.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality submission. Single recoverable date error (March 20 vs March 17). APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS.
